title: hieracium macranthum subsp. testimoniale
intro: Hieracium macranthum subsp. testimoniale, commonly known as the Great Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Hieracium macranthum subsp. testimoniale is often used as an infusion or tincture, and its extracts have been studied for potential use in the development of new medications and treatments.
